title: dampiera haematotricha subsp. dura
intro: Dampiera haematotricha subsp. dura is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, commonly known as a native Australian plant. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Dampiera haematotricha subsp. dura have been reported to offer several health benefits, including wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and potential anticancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential uses.
